message: So last week my mom texted our family group chat at 11 PM: 'Just tested all the smoke alarms. Three need batteries.' Three! And suddenly my siblings and I are all awake, joking about how she's become our emergency preparedness queen, but also... she was right to do it. I realized I couldn't actually remember the last time I'd checked mine. We ended up having this whole thing where everyone agreed to do a quick walk-through of their places. Nothing dramatic—just opening cabinets, looking at batteries, sketching out who goes where if something happens. My brother sent back a selfie with a smoke detector and a thumbs up, which made me laugh, but honestly? That little check-in probably matters more than any of us want to admit. Do you have a smoke alarm buddy in your life—someone who keeps you honest about this stuff?
